records.ety: [OE., boy, servant, knave, AS. cnafa boy, youth; cf. AS. cnapa boy, youth, D. knaap, G. knabe boy, knappe esquire, Icel. knapi, Sw. knape esquire, knäfvel knave.]

defs: A boy; especially, a boy servant.
quotes: O murderous slumber, Lay'st thou thy leaden mace upon my boy That plays thee music ? Gentle knave, good night.
He's but Fortune's knave, A minister of her will.

defs: A tricky, deceitful fellow; a dishonest person; a rogue; a villain.
quotes: In defiance of demonstration, knaves will continue to proselyte fools.
notes: ☞ “How many serving lads must have been unfaithful and dishonest before knave -- which meant at first no more than boy -- acquired the meaning which it has now !”

defs: A playing card marked with the figure of a servant or soldier; a jack; as, the knave of hearts.
